title: Terminate Network Connections After a Defined Period of Inactivity
description: Terminate network connections associated with communications sessions at the end of the sessions or after a defined period of inactivity.

checks.name: RDP Idle Session Timeout is Configured
checks.purpose: Verify RDP MaxIdleTime is configured to 15 minutes (900000 ms) or less via GPO registry to terminate idle remote sessions

checks.name: Screen Lock Timeout is 15 Minutes or Less
checks.purpose: Confirm ScreenSaveTimeOut is set to 900 seconds or less to lock the workstation after inactivity, terminating unattended local sessions

checks.name: RDP Idle Session Timeout is Configured
checks.purpose: Verify RDP MaxIdleTime is configured to 15 minutes or less on the server to terminate idle administrative remote sessions

checks.name: Screen Lock Timeout is 15 Minutes or Less
checks.purpose: Confirm screen lock timeout is 900 seconds or less on the server console

checks.name: Session Timeout Policy Documented
checks.purpose: Verify documented policy defines the maximum inactivity period for all connection types — policy review only

checks.name: SSH ClientAliveInterval is Configured
checks.purpose: Verify ClientAliveInterval and ClientAliveCountMax in sshd_config are set to terminate idle SSH sessions within 15 minutes

checks.name: Shell TMOUT is Set
checks.purpose: Confirm TMOUT is configured in /etc/profile or /etc/profile.d/ to automatically terminate idle interactive shell sessions within 15 minutes

checks.name: SSH ClientAliveInterval is Configured
checks.purpose: Verify ClientAliveInterval and ClientAliveCountMax in sshd_config are set to terminate idle SSH sessions within 15 minutes

checks.name: Shell TMOUT is Set
checks.purpose: Confirm TMOUT is configured in /etc/profile or /etc/profile.d/ to automatically terminate idle interactive shell sessions within 15 minutes

remediation: Configure RDP MaxIdleTime via GPO (Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Remote Desktop Services). Set screen saver timeout to 900 seconds with lock on resume. On Linux, set ClientAliveInterval 300 and ClientAliveCountMax 3 in /etc/ssh/sshd_config, and set TMOUT=900 in /etc/profile.d/timeout.sh.
